message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): watch::Receiver::changed observes pre-cancel (tick 423)

Pins cx-cancel observation on the asupersync watch-channel `changed`
primitive. With the sender alive but no new value published (version
unchanged) and a pre-cancelled cx, `rx.changed(&cx)` must return
`Err(RecvError::Cancelled)` promptly.

Cancel semantics surfaced by asupersync's `poll_changed`:

    if cx.checkpoint().is_err() {
        Poll::Ready(Err(RecvError::Cancelled))
    }

Test shape (matches ticks 419-422):
- `(tx, mut rx) = watch::channel::<u64>(0)` — keep `_tx` alive
- `rx.borrow_and_update()` to mark the initial value as seen (so
  `changed` waits for a new publish rather than returning immediately)
- Pre-cancel cx via `cx.cancel_with(User, ...)`
- Wrap `rx.changed(&cx)` in 2 s outer safety-net timeout
- Assert elapsed < 1 s AND `Err(RecvError::Cancelled)` specifically

Observed locally: test returns in under 10 ms — cx-checkpoint
short-circuits on the first poll.

Long-lived wait primitive cancel matrix now complete for all four
channel types (oneshot/broadcast/mpsc/watch) + semaphore + yield:

| Primitive                      | Budget        | Direct cancel    |
|--------------------------------|---------------|------------------|
| sleep_with_cx                  | ✓ tick 382    | ✗ documented     |
| timeout_with_cx                | ✓ tick 383    | ✗ documented     |
| yield_now_with_cx              | (n/a)         | ✓ tick 418       |
| oneshot_recv_with_cx           | (transitive)  | ✓ tick 419       |
| broadcast_recv_with_cx         | (transitive)  | ✓ tick 420       |
| Semaphore::acquire_with_cx     | (transitive)  | ✓ tick 421       |
| mpsc::Receiver::recv           | (transitive)  | ✓ tick 422       |
| watch::Receiver::changed       | (transitive)  | ✓ tick 423       |

Watch channels underpin config-change notification and
snapshot-version signalling throughout the core — pinning cx-cancel
guards those patterns.

Check script Run 6/6 filter extends to include `watch_changed_with_cx`.
Test counts:

  1/6 HTTP client       : 34
  2/6 TLS               : 45
  3/6 Regression guards :  3
  4/6 Metrics server    :  3
  5/6 Web server        :  2
  6/6 runtime_compat    :  9  (+1 from tick 423)
  total                 : 96

Full check script passes locally via fork bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -15,15 +15,16 @@
 #   4. Metrics server cx-first family (3) — metrics_server_start_with_cx_*
 #      Pre-cancel / mid-flight-cancel / happy path.
 #   5. Web server cx pre-cancel (1) — web_server_with_cx_*
-#   6. runtime_compat primitive contracts (8) — _with_cx_observes_budget_deadline
+#   6. runtime_compat primitive contracts (9) — _with_cx_observes_budget_deadline
 #      + yield_now_with_cx + oneshot_recv_with_cx + broadcast_recv_with_cx
-#      + semaphore_acquire_with_cx + mpsc_recv_with_cx
+#      + semaphore_acquire_with_cx + mpsc_recv_with_cx + watch_changed_with_cx
 #      sleep_with_cx + timeout_with_cx budget-observation tests (ticks
 #      382/383), yield_now_with_cx cancel-checkpoint + happy-path tests
 #      (tick 418), oneshot_recv_with_cx pre-cancel test (tick 419),
 #      broadcast_recv_with_cx pre-cancel test (tick 420),
 #      Semaphore::acquire_with_cx pre-cancel test (tick 421),
-#      mpsc::Receiver::recv pre-cancel test (tick 422).
+#      mpsc::Receiver::recv pre-cancel test (tick 422),
+#      watch::Receiver::changed pre-cancel test (tick 423).
 #
 # Exit code 0 on all passing; non-zero on any failure.
 #
@@ -127,7 +128,7 @@ run_test "Run 6/6: runtime_compat primitive contracts (budget + cancel observati
     -p frankenterm-core \
     --features asupersync-runtime \
     --lib \
-    -- --nocapture _with_cx_observes_budget_deadline yield_now_with_cx oneshot_recv_with_cx broadcast_recv_with_cx semaphore_acquire_with_cx mpsc_recv_with_cx
+    -- --nocapture _with_cx_observes_budget_deadline yield_now_with_cx oneshot_recv_with_cx broadcast_recv_with_cx semaphore_acquire_with_cx mpsc_recv_with_cx watch_changed_with_cx
 
 echo
 echo "=============================================================="
